title: Coding Challenges: Enums, Pattern Matching & Option

challenges.prompt: Define an enum `Direction` with variants `North`, `South`, `East`, `West`. Write a function `opposite(d: Direction) -> Direction` that returns the opposite direction, and a function `direction_name(d: Direction) -> String` that returns the lowercase name string ("north", "south", "east", "west"). Write an entry function `direction_info(name: &str) -> String` that parses name into a Direction (matching "north"/"south"/"east"/"west"), then returns `"opposite of north is south"` format.

Write a function `safe_divide(a: f64, b: f64) -> Option<f64>` that returns `None` if `b` is zero and `Some(a / b)` otherwise. Then write `divide_or_default(a: f64, b: f64, default: f64) -> f64` that calls `safe_divide` and uses `.unwrap_or` to return the default on `None`. Round the result to 2 decimal places (`(v * 100.0).round() / 100.0`). Write an entry function `compute(input: &str) -> String` that parses `"a b default"` (three space-separated f64s) and returns the result as a string.

Write a function `classify_number(n: i64) -> String` that uses `match` to return:
- `"negative"` for any n < 0
- `"zero"` for n == 0
- `"small"` for 1..=9
- `"teen"` for 10..=19
- `"large"` for anything else

Use match with range patterns (`n if n < 0`, `1..=9`, etc.).
